commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=143de2d976ed97d124286b4f8d85b78328590a83)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a zero-hit status helper for `memory_search` forced-sync decisions and updates tests to expect ready indexes to return empty results without retrying.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +52. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main forces sync after zero-hit `memory_search`, and QMD sync awaits update work; the canonical issue also includes live QMD stall reports.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human follow-up is needed because the PR needs contributor-side real behavior proof and maintainer choice between the broader fallback change and the narrower QMD-only fix.

target.title: fix(memory-core): skip forced sync for healthy zero-hit search
target.body:
## Summary
- skip blocking forced sync when memory search returns zero hits but the index is dirty-only and otherwise healthy
- keep forced sync for missing, invalid, or unready memory states
- add regression coverage for dirty-but-ready zero-hit search behavior and disabled vector/FTS healthy modes

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`

Focused output from the latest run:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/lestat/openclaw-fresh/workspace/external/openclaw-source-preservation

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Start at  19:55:35
   Duration  5.33s (transform 2.67s, setup 348ms, import 4.68s, tests 95ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 17.06s
```

## Runtime/Behavior Proof
- The regression test `returns a healthy zero-hit without forcing sync when the index is ready` exercises the tool wrapper path, returns `details.results: []`, asserts `searchCalls === 1`, and asserts `getMemorySyncMockCalls() === 0`.
- Local installed-runtime proof before promotion: live memory status was indexed and ready, and a synthetic valid no-hit returned `0` results in about `491.6ms` with `forcedSync.attempted=false` and `forcedSync.reason=healthy_zero_hit` after gateway reload.
- Current local status check remains healthy: `openclaw memory status --json` reports builtin memory with `files: 159`, `chunks: 2519`, `dirty: false`, `fts.enabled: true`, `fts.available: true`, `vector.enabled: true`, and `custom.indexIdentity.status: valid`.

## Review Follow-up
- Addressed ClawSweeper's disabled-vector finding by only treating `available=false` as unhealthy when the component is enabled or the enabled state is unknown.
- Added explicit decision coverage for `{ vector: { enabled: false, available: false } }` and `{ fts: { enabled: false, available: false } }` returning `healthy_zero_hit`.
target.author: lestat569769
